categories.name: Core Rendering
categories.description: Table rendering, DOM management, and visual states

categories.name: Data Layer
categories.description: Data adapters, datasets, and data fetching

categories.name: Filtering
categories.description: Client-side and server-side text filtering with auto-switching

categories.name: Sorting
categories.description: Client-side and server-side column sorting with multi-column support

categories.name: Web Component
categories.description: Declarative HTML-first API via custom elements

categories.name: Extensibility
categories.description: Plugin system, hooks, events, and developer APIs

categories.name: Planned Plugins
categories.description: Features designed to ship as opt-in plugins

features.name: HTML table rendering
features.description: Renders a full HTML table from a configuration object and data source. Builds thead, tbody, and optional pagination/filter UI.
features.implementation: StreamGrid.js renderHeader(), renderBody(), renderPagination() methods.
features.benefit: Single constructor call produces a complete, interactive data table.

features.name: Loading shimmer state
features.description: Animated shimmer rows with staggered CSS animation delays while data loads.
features.implementation: showLoading() API with sg-loading-row class and CSS @keyframes shimmer. Zero JS animation.
features.benefit: Users see immediate visual feedback — no blank table flash.

features.name: Empty state display
features.description: Shows a configurable message when there are no rows or when filters return no matches.
features.implementation: Two paths: genuine empty dataset vs. active filter with no results. Both use .sg-empty-row. Configurable emptyText option.
features.benefit: Users never see a blank table — they always know why there's no data.

features.name: Column render callbacks
features.description: Per-column render(value, row, context) function that returns string (innerHTML), Node (appendChild), or null (textContent).
features.implementation: render() function in column config with context = { type: 'display', field, col }. onRenderError hook for error handling.
features.benefit: Full control over cell content — badges, links, images, custom HTML — without modifying data.

features.name: Declarative template rendering
features.description: {{value}} and {{row.field}} interpolation in HTML <template> elements, with XSS protection via escapeHtml().
features.implementation: Web component template attribute on <stream-grid> or <stream-grid-column>. Compiles template strings to render functions at init time.
features.benefit: HTML-first developers can customize cell rendering without writing JavaScript.

features.name: REST API adapter
features.description: Connects to any JSON REST API via fetch. Supports custom headers, configurable endpoints, and automatic column inference.
features.implementation: RestApiAdapter extends BaseDataAdapter. Uses query builder functions for server-side pagination, filtering, and sorting.
features.benefit: Drop-in integration with any JSON API — no backend changes needed.

features.name: CacheAdapter (LRU + dedup)
features.description: Wraps any adapter with LRU caching, configurable TTL, per-table TTL overrides, and in-flight request deduplication.
features.implementation: CacheAdapter decorator pattern. Uses stableSerialise for deterministic cache keys. Auto-invalidates on writes.
features.benefit: Reduces API calls dramatically — identical concurrent requests return the same promise.

features.name: In-memory DataSet
features.description: Client-side data store with append, clear, getAll, and setAll operations.
features.implementation: DataSet class wraps an array of row objects.
features.benefit: Clean separation between adapter responses and grid rendering layer.

features.name: Custom adapter support
features.description: Extend BaseDataAdapter to connect to any data source — Firebase, GraphQL, localStorage, or static arrays.
features.implementation: BaseDataAdapter abstract class with getRows/getColumns/insertRow/updateRow/deleteRow contract.
features.benefit: Not locked into REST APIs — works with any backend or client-side data source.

features.name: Client-side pagination
features.description: Three modes: 'pages' (prev/next), 'numbers' (clickable page buttons with sliding window), 'infinite' (load more on scroll).
features.implementation: paginator.js page() and infinite() functions. paginationHelpers.js getPageWindow() for button calculations.
features.benefit: Works with any data source — no server support needed. Three UX patterns in one option.

features.name: Server-side pagination
features.description: Delegates pagination to the server via query parameters. Supports all three pagination modes.
features.implementation: RestApiAdapter passes _page/_limit params to server. Grid reads X-Total-Count header for page calculations.
features.benefit: Handles millions of rows — only the current page is fetched.

features.name: Infinite scroll
features.description: Appends rows as the user scrolls to the bottom. Works in both client-side and server-side modes.
features.implementation: paginationMode: 'infinite'. Intersection Observer or scroll event detection. loadMoreRows() public API.
features.benefit: Modern UX for large datasets — no page buttons, just continuous scrolling.

features.name: Client-side text filtering
features.description: Debounced text input filters rows across all columns using case-insensitive substring matching.
features.implementation: filterEngine.js filterRows() pure function. Configurable debounce delay and filter fields.
features.benefit: Instant, responsive filtering with no server round-trips for small datasets.

features.name: Server-side filtering
features.description: Sends filter text to the server as a query parameter when the dataset exceeds the client-side threshold.
features.implementation: filterMode: 'server' or auto-switching via clientFilterThreshold. Query builder serializes filter state.
features.benefit: Scales to any dataset size — the server handles the heavy lifting.

features.name: Auto-switching filter mode
features.description: Automatically switches between client-side and server-side filtering based on a configurable row threshold.
features.implementation: filterMode: 'auto' (default). clientFilterThreshold determines the crossover point.
features.benefit: Optimal performance without manual configuration — fast for small data, scalable for large data.

features.name: Client-side sorting
features.description: Click column headers to sort. Three-click cycle: ascending → descending → clear.
features.implementation: sortComparators.js with string/number/date comparators. Multi-column via shift-click. Sort priority badges in headers.
features.benefit: Instant, responsive sorting with visual indicators.

features.name: Multi-column sorting
features.description: Shift-click to add secondary, tertiary sort levels. Sort priority badges show the order.
features.implementation: sortStack array of { field, direction } objects. Visual priority indicators on column headers.
features.benefit: Complex data analysis without custom code — sort by category, then by date, then by name.

features.name: Server-side sorting
features.description: Delegates sorting to the server when dataset exceeds client threshold.
features.implementation: sortMode: 'server' or auto-switching via clientSortThreshold. Query builder serializes sort state.
features.benefit: Handles datasets too large for client-side sort.

features.name: Custom sort comparators
features.description: Per-column custom sort function: (a, b) => number.
features.implementation: Column config sorter option accepts 'string', 'number', 'date', or a custom function.
features.benefit: Sort by priority levels, custom formats, or computed values.

features.name: <stream-grid> custom element
features.description: Declarative HTML-first grid via custom element. Maps HTML attributes to StreamGrid options.
features.implementation: StreamGridElement registers <stream-grid>. Light DOM. Attributes: src, table, page-size, pagination-mode, filter-mode, filter-debounce, sort-mode, template. queueMicrotask batching for attribute changes.
features.benefit: Use StreamGrid without writing a single line of JavaScript — just HTML attributes.

features.name: <stream-grid-column> declarative columns
features.description: Declarative column configuration via <stream-grid-column> child elements.
features.implementation: Passive data carrier with field, label, sortable, sorter, width, filter, and template attributes. Notifies parent on changes.
features.benefit: Define columns in HTML — no JavaScript column config array needed.

features.name: Declarative DOM column config
features.description: Read column definitions from existing <th> elements in the DOM using columns: 'dom'.
features.implementation: data-field, data-sg-label, data-sg-sortable, data-sg-sorter, data-sg-width, data-sg-filter attributes on <th> elements.
features.benefit: Progressive enhancement — add StreamGrid to an existing HTML table without rewriting it.

features.name: element.grid escape hatch
features.description: Access the underlying StreamGrid instance from the custom element for programmatic control.
features.implementation: element.grid property returns the StreamGrid instance after initialization.
features.benefit: Start declarative, drop to JavaScript when you need full control.

features.name: Event system
features.description: Lightweight pub/sub events for row click, cell click, header click, page change, filter change, sort change, and loading states.
features.implementation: EventEmitter with on/off/emit. Events: rowClicked, cellClicked, headerClicked, pageChanged, filterChanged, sortChanged, loadingStart, loadingEnd.
features.benefit: React to grid interactions without coupling to DOM events.

features.name: WordPress-style hook system
features.description: addAction/doAction/addFilter/applyFilters with priority ordering for init-time customization.
features.implementation: HookManager class. Plugins register hooks during the init lifecycle.
features.benefit: Familiar API for WordPress developers. Extensible without subclassing.

features.name: Init-time plugin API
features.description: Plugins receive the grid instance at init and can register hooks, events, and modify behavior.
features.implementation: plugins: [MyPlugin] in grid config. Each plugin is a function(grid) called during construction.
features.benefit: First-class extension point — plugins are just functions, no class hierarchy.

features.name: Config export/import
features.description: Serializable snapshot of grid state: columns, filters, pagination, sort. Round-trip safe.
features.implementation: exportConfig() returns plain object with no functions or DOM references. JSON.stringify() safe.
features.benefit: Save and restore grid state — bookmarkable URLs, user preferences, grid presets.

features.name: URL-sync state
features.description: Persist page/filter/sort state in the URL via replaceState for bookmarkable grids.
features.benefit: Users can share or bookmark grid views. Browser back button works naturally.

features.name: Live/real-time polling mode
features.description: paginationMode: 'live' polls the adapter on an interval, diffs rows, and updates only what changed.
features.benefit: Real-time dashboards and monitoring without WebSocket complexity.

features.name: Keyboard navigation
features.description: Arrow keys, Enter for activation, / to focus filter, Escape to clear.
features.benefit: Accessibility moat — most grid libraries have poor keyboard support.

features.name: Row grouping
features.description: groupBy: 'field' creates collapsible group headers in the table.
features.benefit: Organize large datasets visually — group by category, status, or any field.

features.name: Virtual scrolling
features.description: Render only visible rows for massive datasets. Rewrites renderBody() with a viewport approach.
features.benefit: Handle 100,000+ rows without DOM performance degradation.

features.name: Frozen/pinned columns
features.description: pinned: 'left' or 'right' via CSS position: sticky.
features.benefit: Keep key columns visible while scrolling wide tables horizontally.

features.name: Zero-config column type inference
features.description: Infer column types (string, number, date, boolean) from data and auto-choose filter controls.
features.benefit: Smart defaults — range sliders for numbers, date pickers for dates, checkboxes for booleans.

features.name: Diff-aware re-render
features.description: Patch only changed rows instead of full innerHTML wipe. Requires rowKey option.
features.benefit: Preserves DOM state (inputs, selections) and reduces reflow.

features.name: Migration adapter (DataTables/Tabulator)
features.description: fromDataTablesConfig(cfg) converts a DataTables configuration to StreamGrid format.
features.benefit: Zero-friction migration path from legacy grid libraries.

features.name: CSV Export plugin
features.description: Export visible rows to .csv file. Respects current filter and sort state.
features.benefit: One-click data download for end users.

features.name: Inline Editing plugin
features.description: Click to edit cell values directly in the table. Emits edit events for persistence.
features.benefit: Spreadsheet-like editing without leaving the table.

features.name: Column Resize plugin
features.description: Drag column borders to resize. Persists widths via exportConfig().
features.benefit: Users control column widths to fit their content.

features.name: Column Visibility plugin
features.description: Toggle column visibility dynamically via a dropdown or API.
features.benefit: Users see only the columns they care about.

features.name: <stream-grid-toolbar> component
features.description: Declarative toolbar with search, export, column visibility, and refresh controls.
features.benefit: Common grid UI pattern in a single web component.
